repo_prior_art.cite: stat_conformal_overlap_frontier: its ratio-list kernel concerns incompatible candidate target laws, not one common target with finite member-arm orbit selectors.

published_prior_art.cite: WangLiYu2026 gives finite-sample CRT effect prediction and explicitly leaves pair-matching/covariate-adaptive randomization for future work; DobribanYu2025 supplies general group-symmetry prediction; HemerikGoeman2018 supplies the finite-group orbit argument. The finite quotient-measure iff itself is elementary and needs a sharper comparison to weighted conformal calibration.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue proposal gives this same stacked common-arm selector formulation; stat_conformal_overlap_frontier is a different covariate-shift ratio-list project.

published_prior_art.cite: RamdasBarberCandesTibshirani2023 permits arbitrary permutation distributions for tests, while WangLiYu2026 gives CRT conformal effect intervals; the displayed LP feasibility equivalence is direct finite-dimensional mass matching, and the coverage step is the standard exchangeable-rank argument once Theorem 1 is available.

repo_prior_art.cite: No active or banked catalogue entry studies the finite two-arm selector-orbit coverage-deficit functional.

published_prior_art.cite: BarberCandesRamdasTibshirani2023 studies coverage robustness beyond exchangeability and DobribanYu2025 studies group-symmetry prediction, but neither gives the stated two-arm finite-orbit frontier.

structure_flags.label: thm:orbit-balance-iff / thm:common-selector-lp
structure_flags.one_line: The advertised strongest computable kernel is still typed as two unproved theorems; reclassify the headline kernel as a conjecture and retain the exact-frontier OEQ as its open sharpening.

novelty_flags.label: thm:orbit-balance-iff
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey omits TibshiraniBarberCandesRamdas2019, the closest published target-versus-calibration distribution result via weighted exchangeability/covariate-shift conformal prediction; it must be compared explicitly to the orbit-mass certificate.

novelty_flags.label: Stat headline / thm:common-selector-lp
novelty_flags.one_line: As written the claimed coverage procedure is oracle-only, while the LP is a direct balance identity plus a standard rank argument; no implementable new estimator, sharp bound, strict published improvement, or first-result claim currently establishes a field-level Stat advance.

soundness_flags.label: def:rank-inversion and thm:common-selector-lp
soundness_flags.one_line: The algorithm evaluates Y_v(a) and an ell(D;X_v) whose declared input D contains both potential outcomes, although a matched CRT observes only Y_v(Z_v); U_{i,a} is not required to satisfy Z_v=a, so the proposed calibration scores and intervals are not observable-data statistics.

soundness_flags.label: thm:common-selector-lp
soundness_flags.one_line: The coverage clause invokes unspecified 'rank conditions'; the only formal ingredients are the order-statistic definition and exchangeability, so state the precise tie/rank convention and its sufficient assumptions.

soundness_flags.label: ass:calibration-selector-product-law; ass:calibration-selector-array-independence; ass:generic-selector-array-independence
soundness_flags.one_line: Independent external draws of member-arm states make sense only with complete potential outcomes; in an actual CRT an observable arm-a score selection must depend on the realized assignment recorded in D, contradicting the asserted independence without an observed-data redesign.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: technical_internal_limitation / replicated-array regime
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The guarantee may be conditional on iid replicated matched-block arrays, an externally selected target, and prespecified weights; it is not a design-based guarantee for Shamba Maisha's one fixed trial.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any eventual coverage theorem is restricted to that replicated-superpopulation regime, after the selector construction is replaced by an observable-data one.

source_verification_receipts.version: Statistica Sinica accepted author version, SS-2025-0476
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: It constructs finite-sample cluster and individual treatment-effect prediction intervals with arbitrary working outcome models, assumes simple cluster randomization, and identifies pair-matching/covariate-adaptive randomization as future work.
SymmPI gives predictive inference under general group distributional invariance using distributionally equivariant transformations and includes distribution-shift guarantees.
The paper formalizes finite-group permutation-invariance conditions and derives exact level control through group-orbit arguments.
A randomized modification gives valid permutation p-values for arbitrary, possibly nonuniform distributions on arbitrary permutation subsets.

source_verification_receipts.locator: Abstract and nonexchangeable split-conformal coverage bounds
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper develops weighted-quantile and randomized conformal procedures with quantitative robustness guarantees beyond exchangeability.
It studies matched-pair cluster randomization, asymptotic inference for weighted difference-in-means estimators, and finite-sample validity of a randomization test for specified nulls.
The Shamba Maisha cluster randomized trial was conducted in eight pairs of Kenyan health facilities.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace complete-record D by an observed-data array, define calibration selection jointly with the realized assignment and its randomization law, restrict the learner to the observed projection, and rederive the rank argument under that law.

Promote the orbit certificate to a conjecture explicitly framed as a strict extension of WangLiYu2026 from simple randomization to a named pair-matched assignment regime; add the exact section anchor and compare it with TibshiraniBarberCandesRamdas2019 weighted exchangeability.

Turn the frontier OEQ into a field-grade result by giving a non-disjoint explicit score-law construction and either an exact formula for Gamma or a sharp strict improvement over the generic total-variation coverage bound.
Correct bibliography metadata for DobribanYu2025 (JRSSB 87(5), 1353-1381) and BaiLiuShaikhTabordMeehan2024 (Journal of Econometrics 245, 105873).

recommended_next_step: Revise the kernel around an observable matched-randomization selector and a conjectural, benchmarked strict extension; do not advance until the oracle-score defect and Stat-frontier gap are resolved.
